type.description: Type of waypoint. Flight plans usually start with 'origin', followed by 'departure_runway'. Flight plans usually end with 'destination_runway', followed by 'destination'

name.description: ICAO identifier for airports, name of navaids, free text for any other type of waypoint. Uppercased

longitude.description: Easting, using the World Geodetic System 1984 (WGS 84) [WGS84] datum, with longitude and latitude units of decimal degrees; [-180,180]

latitude.description: Northing, using the World Geodetic System 1984 (WGS 84) [WGS84] datum, with longitude and latitude units of decimal degrees; [-90,90]

altitude.description: The height in meters above or below the WGS 84 reference ellipsoid

flyOver.description: Whether to fly over the waypoint

name.description: Lowercased identifier for Aerofly FS4 aircraft, e.g. "c172". The current list of Aerofly FS4 aircraft codes can be obtained from https://fboes.github.io/aerofly-data/data/aircraft.html or https://fboes.github.io/aerofly-data/data/aircraft-liveries.json

livery.description: Lowercased identifier for Aerofly FS4 paint scheme for given aircraft. The current list of Aerofly FS4 paint schemes can be obtained from https://fboes.github.io/aerofly-data/data/aircraft-liveries.json

callsign.description: Callsign of aircraft. This string is supposed to be uppercased. Default to an empty string.

fuelMass.description: Fuel mass in kg. The potential maximum fuel mass can be obtained from https://fboes.github.io/aerofly-data/data/aircraft-liveries.json.

payloadMass.description: Payload mass in kg. The potential maximum payload mass can be obtained from https://fboes.github.io/aerofly-data/data/aircraft-liveries.json.

longitude.description: Easting, using the World Geodetic System 1984 (WGS 84) [WGS84] datum, with longitude and latitude units of decimal degrees; [-180,180]

latitude.description: Northing, using the World Geodetic System 1984 (WGS 84) [WGS84] datum, with longitude and latitude units of decimal degrees; [-90,90]

alt.description: The height in meters above or below the WGS 84 reference ellipsoid

longitude.description: Easting, using the World Geodetic System 1984 (WGS 84) [WGS84] datum, with longitude and latitude units of decimal degrees; [-180,180]

latitude.description: Northing, using the World Geodetic System 1984 (WGS 84) [WGS84] datum, with longitude and latitude units of decimal degrees; [-90,90]

alt.description: The height in meters above or below the WGS 84 reference ellipsoid

cover.description: Cloud cover as normalized value [0,1], where 0 = clear, 1 = overcast

base.description: Cloud base height in meters above WGS 84 reference ellipsoid

time.description: UTC time and day, given as ISO 8601 string, e.g. "2024-05-28T11:00:00Z"

direction.description: Wind direction where the wind is coming from, in degrees
